member.name: T:DetMath.DetMathf
member.summary: Deterministic math functions for Fix64. Mirrors Unity's Mathf — call DetMathf.X instead of Mathf.X.

member.name: M:DetMath.DetMathf.Abs(DetMath.Fix64)
member.summary: Returns the absolute value. Example: Abs(−3.00) → 3.00

member.name: M:DetMath.DetMathf.Min(DetMath.Fix64,DetMath.Fix64)
member.summary: Returns the lesser of two values.

member.name: M:DetMath.DetMathf.Max(DetMath.Fix64,DetMath.Fix64)
member.summary: Returns the greater of two values.

member.name: M:DetMath.DetMathf.Clamp(DetMath.Fix64,DetMath.Fix64,DetMath.Fix64)
member.summary: Clamps to [, ]. Use Clamp(v, Fix64.Zero, Fix64.One) for a 0–1 range.

member.name: M:DetMath.DetMathf.Lerp(DetMath.Fix64,DetMath.Fix64,DetMath.Fix64)
member.summary: Linearly interpolates from to . is clamped to [0, 1] — use for smooth transitions. Example: Lerp(2, 8, 0.50) → 5.00

member.name: M:DetMath.DetMathf.Ratio(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Shorthand for Fix64.FromRatio(n, d). Example: Ratio(3, 2) → 1.50

member.name: T:DetMath.DetVector2
member.summary: Immutable 2D vector using Fix64 components. Use for directions, velocities, and influence calculations in simulation logic.

member.name: M:DetMath.DetVector2.#ctor(DetMath.Fix64,DetMath.Fix64)
member.summary: Creates a 2D vector with the given components.

member.name: P:DetMath.DetVector2.X
member.summary: Horizontal component.

member.name: P:DetMath.DetVector2.Y
member.summary: Vertical component.

member.name: P:DetMath.DetVector2.LengthSquared
member.summary: X² + Y² — squared length of the vector. Use to compare distances without needing Sqrt.

member.name: M:DetMath.DetVector2.Dot(DetMath.DetVector2,DetMath.DetVector2)
member.summary: Returns the dot product of two vectors. Result is 1.00 when parallel, 0.00 when perpendicular, −1.00 when opposite. Use to measure alignment between a unit's facing direction and a target direction.

member.name: T:DetMath.Fix64
member.summary: Deterministic fixed-point number with 2 decimal places. All arithmetic uses long integers — no float, no double. Identical results on every platform (x86, ARM, etc.). Safe multiplication range: ±30,370,004.

member.name: F:DetMath.Fix64.Precision
member.summary: Internal scale factor. 1 unit = 100 raw.

member.name: P:DetMath.Fix64.RawValue
member.summary: Internal integer representation. Divide by to get the real value. Use for display only: (float)value.RawValue / Fix64.Precision

member.name: M:DetMath.Fix64.FromRaw(System.Int64)
member.summary: Creates a Fix64 directly from a raw internal value. Only use when you know the raw value explicitly.

member.name: M:DetMath.Fix64.FromInt(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a Fix64 from a whole integer. Example: FromInt(3) → 3.00

member.name: M:DetMath.Fix64.FromRatio(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a Fix64 from a ratio. Example: FromRatio(3, 2) → 1.50 Prefer this over float literals. Truncates toward zero if not exact.

member.name: M:DetMath.Fix64.ToIntFloor
member.summary: Converts to the largest integer less than or equal to this value (floor toward −∞). Example: −1.50 → −2. Use for completed cycle counts.

member.name: M:DetMath.Fix64.ToIntTruncate
member.summary: Converts to integer by discarding the fractional part (truncate toward zero). Example: −1.50 → −1.

member.name: M:DetMath.Fix64.ToString
member.summary: Returns the value formatted as a decimal string. Example: "1.50" Use for display and logging only — do not parse back into Fix64.

member.name: T:DetMath.GridPoint
member.summary: Immutable integer coordinate on a tile grid. Use for building positions, unit positions, and path cost calculations.

member.name: M:DetMath.GridPoint.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a grid coordinate at (x, y).

member.name: P:DetMath.GridPoint.X
member.summary: Horizontal tile coordinate.

member.name: P:DetMath.GridPoint.Y
member.summary: Vertical tile coordinate.

member.name: M:DetMath.GridPoint.ManhattanDistance(DetMath.GridPoint)
member.summary: Returns the number of steps to reach moving in 4 directions only (up/down/left/right). Use for road networks and grid-based pathfinding. Example: (3,7) → (8,2) = 10

member.name: M:DetMath.GridPoint.ChebyshevDistance(DetMath.GridPoint)
member.summary: Returns the number of steps to reach moving in 8 directions (including diagonal). Use for unit movement where diagonal costs the same as cardinal. Example: (3,7) → (8,2) = 5
